The {{nihongo|'''Nishidani Kofun cluster'''|西谷墳墓群||}} is a group of late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} to early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Otsu-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/207093|title= 西谷墳墓群|trans-title= Nishidani Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishidani tumulus cluster is located on the Nishidani Hills facing the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|WD=Q11500714|lt=Hii River}} at an elevation of 40 meters, in the southeastern part of Izumo city. It consists of six {{nihongo|''Yosumi-tosshutsu-gata''|四隅突出形}}-style tumuli. This is a variation of the {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus with polygonal-shaped protrusions on each of its four corners, a shape unique to the San'in region, as well as a number of other tumuli including 17 square {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=''hōfun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and 4 circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} tombs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1953, a large amount of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery}} was discovered near what was later designated Tomb No. 4, by students of Izumo City Daiichi Junior High School and the existence of the ruins became clear for the first time. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have so far confirmed a total of 32 graves, including 27 Kofuns, 5 earthen pit graves, and 5 {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} graves. These were built from the Yayoi period into the Kofun period, and the ones that attracted particular attention were Tombs 1 to 4, Tomb 6, and Tomb 9, which were built on the hills to the west and north, all of which were four-cornered mounds. Tomb No. 3 and Tomb No. 9 are particularly large, measuring 50 meters including the protruding parts, and are thought to be the tombs of the kings who ruled this region in the late Yayoi period. In the main part of Tomb No. 3, a double-layered wooden coffin made from a large amount of mercury vermilion was excavated from two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} including ''[[magatama]]'', cylindrical and spherical beads, and iron swords were found inside these coffins. Traces of the burial pit being backfilled and four gigantic pillars erected have also been found, indicating that funerary rituals using a large amount of earthenware were performed. In addition, the grave goods in Tombs 3 and 4 include a style of trapezoid pottery unique to the [[Kingdom of Kibi|Kibi region]] ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} and eastern {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}), indicating that there was trade with the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of Japan. That seems to be the case. The site is near the [[Imaichi Dainenji Kofun]] and the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is maintained and opened to the public as the Nishitani Tomb Group Historical Site Park "Izumo Yayoi no Mori" (西谷墳墓群史跡公園・出雲弥生の森), located approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves}} *[https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/373 Izumo Tourism] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20651 Shimane Prefecture Tourism Federation] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves]][[en:Nishidani Tumulus cluster]][[ja:西谷墳墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves]][[en:Nishidani Tumulus cluster]][[ja:西谷墳墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves]][[en:Nishidani Tumulus cluster]][[ja:西谷墳墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nishidani Kofun cluster | native_name =西谷墳墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Nishidani Garves 01.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishidani Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishidani Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|31|N|132|46|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Park, Museum | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|21|31|N|132|46|48|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nishidani Kofun cluster'''|西谷墳墓群||}} is a group of late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} to early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Otsu-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/207093|title= 西谷墳墓群|trans-title= Nishidani Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishidani tumulus cluster is located on the Nishidani Hills facing the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|WD=Q11500714|lt=Hii River}} at an elevation of 40 meters, in the southeastern part of Izumo city. It consists of six {{nihongo|''Yosumi-tosshutsu-gata''|四隅突出形}}-style tumuli. This is a variation of the {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus with polygonal-shaped protrusions on each of its four corners, a shape unique to the San'in region, as well as a number of other tumuli including 17 square {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=''hōfun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and 4 circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} tombs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1953, a large amount of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery}} was discovered near what was later designated Tomb No. 4, by students of Izumo City Daiichi Junior High School and the existence of the ruins became clear for the first time. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have so far confirmed a total of 32 graves, including 27 Kofuns, 5 earthen pit graves, and 5 {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} graves. These were built from the Yayoi period into the Kofun period, and the ones that attracted particular attention were Tombs 1 to 4, Tomb 6, and Tomb 9, which were built on the hills to the west and north, all of which were four-cornered mounds. Tomb No. 3 and Tomb No. 9 are particularly large, measuring 50 meters including the protruding parts, and are thought to be the tombs of the kings who ruled this region in the late Yayoi period. In the main part of Tomb No. 3, a double-layered wooden coffin made from a large amount of mercury vermilion was excavated from two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} including ''[[magatama]]'', cylindrical and spherical beads, and iron swords were found inside these coffins. Traces of the burial pit being backfilled and four gigantic pillars erected have also been found, indicating that funerary rituals using a large amount of earthenware were performed. In addition, the grave goods in Tombs 3 and 4 include a style of trapezoid pottery unique to the [[Kingdom of Kibi|Kibi region]] ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} and eastern {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}), indicating that there was trade with the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of Japan. That seems to be the case. The site is near the [[Imaichi Dainenji Kofun]] and the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is maintained and opened to the public as the Nishitani Tomb Group Historical Site Park "Izumo Yayoi no Mori" (西谷墳墓群史跡公園・出雲弥生の森), located approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves}} *[https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/373 Izumo Tourism] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20651 Shimane Prefecture Tourism Federation] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves]][[en:Nishidani Tumulus cluster]][[ja:西谷墳墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves]][[en:Nishidani Tumulus cluster]][[ja:西谷墳墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves]][[en:Nishidani Tumulus cluster]][[ja:西谷墳墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nishidani Kofun cluster | native_name =西谷墳墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Nishidani Garves 01.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishidani Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishidani Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|31|N|132|46|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Park, Museum | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|21|31|N|132|46|48|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nishidani Kofun cluster'''|西谷墳墓群||}} is a group of late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Otsu-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/207093|title= 西谷墳墓群|trans-title= Nishidani Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishidani tumulus cluster is located on the Nishidani Hills facing the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|WD=Q11500714|lt=Hii River|1=Hii River}} at an elevation of 40 meters, in the southeastern part of Izumo city. It consists of six {{nihongo|''Yosumi-tosshutsu-gata''|四隅突出形}}-style tumuli. This is a variation of the {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus with polygonal-shaped protrusions on each of its four corners, a shape unique to the San'in region, as well as a number of other tumuli including 17 square {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=''hōfun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and 4 circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} tombs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1953, a large amount of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}} was discovered near what was later designated Tomb No. 4, by students of Izumo City Daiichi Junior High School and the existence of the ruins became clear for the first time. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have so far confirmed a total of 32 graves, including 27 Kofuns, 5 earthen pit graves, and 5 {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} graves. These were built from the Yayoi period into the Kofun period, and the ones that attracted particular attention were Tombs 1 to 4, Tomb 6, and Tomb 9, which were built on the hills to the west and north, all of which were four-cornered mounds. Tomb No. 3 and Tomb No. 9 are particularly large, measuring 50 meters including the protruding parts, and are thought to be the tombs of the kings who ruled this region in the late Yayoi period. In the main part of Tomb No. 3, a double-layered wooden coffin made from a large amount of mercury vermilion was excavated from two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} including ''[[magatama]]'', cylindrical and spherical beads, and iron swords were found inside these coffins. Traces of the burial pit being backfilled and four gigantic pillars erected have also been found, indicating that funerary rituals using a large amount of earthenware were performed. In addition, the grave goods in Tombs 3 and 4 include a style of trapezoid pottery unique to the [[Kingdom of Kibi|Kibi region]] ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} and eastern {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}), indicating that there was trade with the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of Japan. That seems to be the case. The site is near the [[Imaichi Dainenji Kofun]] and the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is maintained and opened to the public as the Nishitani Tomb Group Historical Site Park "Izumo Yayoi no Mori" (西谷墳墓群史跡公園・出雲弥生の森), located approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

[[file:Nishidani Graves 08.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Nishidani kofun cluster]] <!-- no translated page template found in history --> [[en:Nishidani Kofun cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q11629514}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yayoi period]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves]][[en:Nishidani Tumulus cluster]][[ja:西谷墳墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves]][[en:Nishidani Tumulus cluster]][[ja:西谷墳墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves]][[en:Nishidani Tumulus cluster]][[ja:西谷墳墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nishidani Kofun cluster | native_name =西谷墳墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Nishidani Garves 01.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishidani Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishidani Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|31|N|132|46|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Park, Museum | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|21|31|N|132|46|48|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nishidani Kofun cluster'''|西谷墳墓群||}} is a group of late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to early [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Otsu-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/207093|title= 西谷墳墓群|trans-title= Nishidani Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishidani tumulus cluster is located on the Nishidani Hills facing the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|WD=Q11500714|lt=Hii River|1=Hii River}} at an elevation of 40 meters, in the southeastern part of Izumo city. It consists of six {{nihongo|''Yosumi-tosshutsu-gata''|四隅突出形}}-style tumuli. This is a variation of the {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square tumulus with polygonal-shaped protrusions on each of its four corners, a shape unique to the San'in region, as well as a number of other tumuli including 17 square {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=''hōfun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and 4 circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} tombs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In 1953, a large amount of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}} was discovered near what was later designated Tomb No. 4, by students of Izumo City Daiichi Junior High School and the existence of the ruins became clear for the first time. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have so far confirmed a total of 32 graves, including 27 Kofuns, 5 earthen pit graves, and 5 {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} graves. These were built from the Yayoi period into the Kofun period, and the ones that attracted particular attention were Tombs 1 to 4, Tomb 6, and Tomb 9, which were built on the hills to the west and north, all of which were four-cornered mounds. Tomb No. 3 and Tomb No. 9 are particularly large, measuring 50 meters including the protruding parts, and are thought to be the tombs of the kings who ruled this region in the late Yayoi period. In the main part of Tomb No. 3, a double-layered wooden coffin made from a large amount of mercury vermilion was excavated from two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} including ''[[magatama]]'', cylindrical and spherical beads, and iron swords were found inside these coffins. Traces of the burial pit being backfilled and four gigantic pillars erected have also been found, indicating that funerary rituals using a large amount of earthenware were performed. In addition, the grave goods in Tombs 3 and 4 include a style of trapezoid pottery unique to the [[Kingdom of Kibi|Kibi region]] ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} and eastern {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}), indicating that there was trade with the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of Japan. That seems to be the case. The site is near the [[Imaichi Dainenji Kofun]] and the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is maintained and opened to the public as the Nishitani Tomb Group Historical Site Park "Izumo Yayoi no Mori" (西谷墳墓群史跡公園・出雲弥生の森), located approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Nishidani Ancient Graves}} *[https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/373 Izumo Tourism] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20651 Shimane Prefecture Tourism Federation] {{in lang|ja}}
